name: Trigger-Event Selling
summary: A prospecting technique that prioritizes outreach around events that make change more likely, such as new leaders, funding, expansion, regulation, or system change.
whenToUseIt: Use trigger-event selling when timing matters and when a buyer is more likely to act because something in their environment has changed recently.
steps: Identify a signal that could credibly increase urgency or change appetite.
Connect the trigger to a relevant business hypothesis, not just a mention of the news.
Use the trigger to justify why this account and why now.
example: A company expands into a new region and adds new operational complexity. The rep reaches out with a point of view on how that expansion usually strains forecasting and manager inspection.
